The 3D Printed 45 RPM Adapters That Every Vinyl DJ Actually Wants

April 6, 2025•5 min read

The Humble 45 Adapter — And Why Most of Them Are Terrible

If you've been collecting 7-inch singles or spinning 45s at a gig, you already know the frustration: those tiny plastic spiders that came with your grandma's record player are brittle, they wobble, and they make your records look like they belong in a thrift store bin. Most cheap adapters slip, scratch, and crack within months.

But there's a better option — and it's been designed by someone who actually plays records.

Meet the "I'm Spinning It / Fade In Out" 3D Printed 45 RPM Adapters

These adapters are handmade in New York using high-quality PETG and PLA filaments, with a steel ring insert for durability and stability. They're not just functional — they're a statement piece for anyone who takes their vinyl seriously.

Each set includes two adapters, designed as a matched pair with two iconic phrases from DJ culture:

  • "FADE IN N OUT" — bold lightning bolt design that references the fundamental move of any DJ set
  • "I'M SPINNING IT" — a golden arch declaration of intent, in the same unapologetic pop-culture style

The designs are printed in red, black, and gold — filling the face of the adapter with visuals that look intentional rather than incidental. These are adapters you'll leave on display.

See It In Action

What Makes These Better Than Standard Adapters

Precision 3D Printing

Each adapter is printed with tight tolerances — no wobble, no rattle. The fit on a standard turntable spindle is snug and centered, which matters more than most people realize. A loose adapter introduces micro-vibrations that degrade sound quality and accelerate stylus wear.

Rubber Grip Rings

Each adapter includes rubber rings that add friction between the adapter and the record. This reduces slippage on the platter and dampens vibration — two problems that cheaper adapters completely ignore.

Steel Ring Insert

The center steel ring gives these adapters longevity that pure plastic adapters can't match. The spindle hole stays perfectly circular even after hundreds of uses.

Ships from New York

Shorter domestic shipping distances mean your order arrives faster without unnecessary transit time or import complications.

Who Is This For?

  • Vinyl DJs who spin 45s and want accessories that match their aesthetic
  • Record collectors who want something more interesting than a generic plastic spider
  • Gift buyers looking for a vinyl-related present that's genuinely useful and under $35
  • DJs who post content — these photograph extremely well for record-spinning reels and unboxing shots

Personalization Available

Want your name, DJ handle, or a custom message added? Personalization is available for a small add-on fee. This makes the set a genuinely one-of-a-kind gift for a DJ friend, or a branded accessory for your own setup.

The Verdict

At $30 for a set of two, these adapters cost about the same as a fast-food meal and last significantly longer. They solve a real problem (adapter instability), look great doing it, and come with the kind of design detail that shows the maker actually uses turntables.

Get them on Etsy: [3D Printed 45RPM Adapters — "I'm Spinning It / Fade In Out"](https://www.etsy.com/listing/4313262158/im-spinning-it-fade-in-out-3d-printed)
